entry.title: What's New in Business Network for Supply Chain - 2025 Q1 Release
entry.content:
SAP Business Network for Supply Chain

Discover the Latest in SAP Business Network for Supply Chain: 2025 Q1 Update. Dive into our newest blog post, "What’s New in SAP Business Network for Supply Chain" for 2025. This article focuses on the exciting features introduced in the 2025 Q1 Release. It's tailored for those looking for an in-depth look at the improvements launched in early 2025 for SAP Business Network for Supply Chain. Below, you'll find a summary of the updates expected to enhance our customers' experience. 

1. Streamlining Compliance with Quality Inspection Certificates in ASN Uploads 

In today’s supply chain landscape, ensuring product quality and compliance is more critical than ever — especially as companies work to meet sustainability goals, regulatory standards, and industry-specific requirements. For suppliers managing quality-controlled materials and buyers ordering such materials, we’ve introduced two new features that simplify the process of handling quality certificates: 

  • Mass Upload for Quality Documents: In addition to adding quality certificates during ASN creation, suppliers can now upload quality certificates during the ASN mass upload at the line-item level. This functionality reduces manual effort, improves data accuracy, and allows for faster processing by enabling bulk certificate uploads. 
  • Buyer Control to Mandate Required Documents: On the buyer side, organizations can specify exactly which quality certifications are needed for each purchase order. This empowers buyers to ensure that only the required certificates are mandatory, helping to streamline the procurement process and enforce compliance standards. 

By simplifying these two processes, businesses can more effectively track critical certifications, such as: 

  • Sustainability credentials and carbon footprint documentation 
  • Compliance with environmental or safety regulations 
  • Industry-specific certifications ensuring product integrity and regulatory adherence.  

Key Benefits: 

  • Improved Compliance: Reduces the risk of missing important certificates, supporting regulatory, sustainability, and industry-specific requirements. 
  • Enhanced Process Adoption: Encourages smoother adoption of internal processes for ship notices and quality certificates across the supply chain. 
  • Improved Traceability: Ensures certificates are only mandatory if specified in the PO, improving traceability and reducing the need for updates before goods receipt generation. 

This feature not only simplifies supplier collaboration but also empowers organizations to maintain stronger oversight and meet their broader environmental, compliance, and quality assurance goals with greater confidence.  

 

 

2. Batch-managed Material Validation in ASN Creation  

Ensuring the accuracy of batch information is crucial for compliance and traceability, especially for certain industries with batch-managed products. To support this, we’ve introduced two key enhancements that improve data integrity for batch-managed materials. 

  • Mandatory Supplier Batch ID: The Supplier Batch ID field can now be marked as mandatory based on a flag in the purchase order item. 
  • Improved Validation for Production and Expiry Dates: Enhanced validation checks for production and expiry dates ensure accuracy when handling batch-managed materials. 

Historically, while SAP Business Network supported batch information in various transactions, there was no mechanism in place to ensure mandatory batch entry during ASN creation. This update addresses that gap, making batch information mandatory where required, ensuring better traceability and reducing the need for updates before goods receipt generation. 

These enhancements offer greater control over batch-managed materials, ensuring that suppliers provide the required information and buyers can maintain full traceability and data integrity across their supply chain. 

3. Qualtrics Survey: Enhancing Feedback Collection for Continuous Improvement  

To better understand user needs and improve our platform, we've expanded on a previous feature to make providing feedback easier and more accessible. Supplier users can now share their thoughts during the Purchase Order, Order Confirmation, and Ship Notice creation processes through a Qualtrics survey. 

The feedback process is streamlined in two ways: 

  • A side tab allows users to voluntarily click and provide input. Once clicked, a pop-up prompts users to share their thoughts. 
  • We’ve introduced a push survey mechanism, which randomly prompts users to provide feedback at time-spaced intervals, without requiring them to click on the feedback tab. This makes it easier to offer input without disrupting the user's workflow. 

This feedback is optional, and users also have the option to sign up for additional research sessions to provide more detailed input to our UX and Product Management teams. 

4. Ensuring Accurate Serial Number Validation in Component Consumption  

To improve material traceability and reduce errors in subcontracting processes, we’ve introduced a feature that allows buyers to require contract manufacturers to select serial numbers directly from their component inventory during component consumption. This ensures that contract manufacturers can only select serial numbers that are available, ensuring only valid components are used. This is particularly critical in certain industries like life sciences, where precise traceability is essential to prevent costly product recalls. 

The feature is composed of two parts: 

  • New Transaction Rule for Serial Number Validation: Buyers can enable a transaction rule that ensures contract manufacturers select only serial numbers from available inventory. 
  • Enhanced UI for Serial Number Selection: In the UI, suppliers can select serial numbers from the Consumed Component Serial Number pop-up, with an “Autofill Serial Number” feature to speed up the process.  

The validation is also supported in B2B and Excel upload scenarios. 

Key Benefits:  

  • Improved Traceability: Ensures only valid serial numbers are used, preventing mistakes and enhancing traceability and compliance and ensuring accurate inventory tracking. 
  • Enhanced Control for Buyers: Buyers can enforce serial number validation to maintain control over material consumption and ensure compliance. 
  • Improved Parent-Child Material Traceability: The system’s handling of the parent-child relationship ensures proper linking of serial numbers, ensuring accurate tracking for related components. 

5. Efficient Management of ASN and Handling Unit (Packaging Materials) Number Ranges  

Managing ASN and Handling Unit Number ranges can be cumbersome, especially when dealing with a large supplier base. For customers who need to create number ranges for individual suppliers, this can result in a long list of entries that are difficult to navigate — particularly when a customer has hundreds or thousands of suppliers. Additionally, pagination can make it harder to find specific entries, requiring users to click through multiple pages. 

To address this, we’ve introduced a feature that enhances the management of number ranges by enabling faster searches based on two new parameters: Supplier Name and Network ID. This streamlines the process for businesses that need to manage many number range configurations. 

Key Benefits: 

  • Faster Search and Enhanced Navigation: Enables faster access to specific number range configurations and reduces the need to click through multiple pages. 
  • Improved Management of Supplier Relationships: Ideal for businesses that require unique number ranges for each supplier, making it easier to manage and maintain complex setups. 

This new feature makes managing ASN and Handling Unit Number ranges more efficient, saving time and improving usability for customers handling large numbers of supplier configurations. 

6. Expanded Custom Attributes for Enhanced Planning Collaboration  

Every industry has unique and specific planning criteria, and to support this, we’ve increased the number of customer-defined attributes for planning records to 60 for both buyers and suppliers. This enhancement offers greater flexibility to capture the data that matters most to your business, enabling more tailored and detailed planning collaboration. 

The Excel upload/download feature also supports up to 30 additional columns for custom attributes, requiring the latest versions of Excel for compatibility. 

Key Benefits: 

  • More Flexibility: Capture up to 60 custom attributes and 30 additional Excel columns to meet industry-specific planning needs. 
  • Better Collaboration: Share and manage more relevant, detailed data between buyers and suppliers. 

7. Improved Planning and Order Tracking with New Key Figures and Flexibility for Supplier Part ID

To help buyers and suppliers stay on top of their order quantities, we’ve introduced two new key figures: Open Order and Open Replenishment Order. These key figures show the net quantity of orders that have yet to be received within a specific time period. This is calculated by subtracting the total quantity of goods receipts from the total quantity of orders with a requested delivery date. 

The new key figures allow buyers and suppliers to easily see what is still pending, improving visibility and planning. 

Key Benefits: 

  • Quick Overview: Gives a clear view of remaining orders that still need to be received based on the original order quantity and what has already been delivered. 
  • Better Planning: These key figures can be used in other calculations, like Projected Stock, to improve decision-making for both buyers and suppliers. 

Additionally, we’ve introduced a new setting that enables purchase orders to be matched using only Buyer Part IDs and Plant IDs, ignoring Supplier Part IDs. This is useful for organizations where Supplier Part IDs frequently change or are not immediately available, ensuring more flexible and reliable transaction matching and ultimately a smoother planning process.  

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Please visit https://www.sap.com/businessnetwork to learn more about SAP Business Network.

To explore SAP Business Network for Supply Chain, visit SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration

For updates on our community and activities, please visit the: SAP Business Network Community

SAP Business Network for Procurement and SAP Business Network for Supply Chain

entry.title: Re: Translation of costing sheet entry
entry.content:
Dear user,

And thanks for providing more details.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the "Define Costing Sheets" configuration app (used to set up overhead rules and costing sheet components) does not offer built-in support for multilingual translation directly within the app UI.

Current limitations:

  • The descriptions for costing sheets, overhead types, and credits are entered in one language only (typically the system language).
  • There is no dedicated translation function in the "Define Costing Sheets" app, unlike other areas (like Fiori Apps for custom fields or texts).

Work around solution : manual entry in each language variant:

  • You can switch your logon language in SAP to another (e.g., from English to German).
  • Then manually re-enter the text fields in the new language.
  • The system saves these as language-dependent texts if supported — but for costing sheets, this functionality is limited.

I hope my explanation is clear enough and answer your main question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

I'm also having issues trying to understand which RBP controls which UI element.

A first baby step goals would be to understand what's on Employee on Self, and Employee on All roles, and start deciding which fields or permissions are needed.

Currently we have overlapping permissions all over the place.

I've been reading, and a Building Blocks Approach is recommended, just looking for tips on how to implement this on a chaotic environment with No RBPs logic so far.

Any guidance is welcome.

entry.title: Re: Completely deleting a material
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you cannot completely delete a material master record once it's been created — especially if it's been used in any transactions (e.g. stock movements, POs, etc.). This is a standard restriction to preserve data consistency.

However, here’s what you can do:

If the material has not been used in any transaction:

  • You can mark it for deletion using the “Manage Product Master Data” app:
    1. Open the material.
    2. Go to the relevant views (e.g., plant, sales org, etc.).
    3. Set the Deletion Flags at all levels (client, plant, storage location, etc.).

Important : Even with all deletion flags, you still cannot reuse the same material number later in S/4HANA Cloud Public.

If the material has been used:

  • The material becomes part of permanent transactional history and cannot be deleted or reused.
  • The system will prevent re-creation of a material with the same number, even if deletion flags are set.

I hope my explanation is clear enough and answer your main question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: ABAP utility to print and view PO Adobe form developed by Form Data Provider (Gateway Model)
entry.content:
REPORT zmm_po_adobe_form.
DATA lo_hlp          TYPE REF TO if_apoc_or_h_factory_internal.
DATA lo_fp_srv       TYPE REF TO if_apoc_somu_form_services.
DATA lo_merge        TYPE REF TO if_apoc_document_merge.
DATA lo_util         TYPE REF TO if_apoc_utility.

DATA lt_master       TYPE if_apoc_common_api=>ty_gt_fdp_parameter.
DATA lt_appl         TYPE if_apoc_common_api=>ty_gt_fdp_parameter.
DATA lt_data         TYPE if_apoc_logger=>tty_ty_data.
DATA lt_msg          TYPE cl_somu_form_services=>ty_gt_message.
DATA lt_msg_all      TYPE cl_somu_form_services=>ty_gt_message.

DATA ls_merge_doc    TYPE if_apoc_document_merge=>document_object.
DATA ls_symsg        TYPE symsg.

DATA lv_mform_cnt_id TYPE apoc_form_master_content_id.
DATA lv_xml_in       TYPE xstring.
DATA lv_xml_out      TYPE xstring.
DATA lv_xml_out_all  TYPE xstring.
DATA lv_appl_multi   TYPE boole_d.
DATA lv_appl_key_chg TYPE boole_d.
DATA lv_stop         TYPE boole_d.
DATA lv_msg          TYPE string.
DATA lv_pdf          TYPE boole_d.
DATA lv_pdl_type     TYPE apoc_pdl_type.
DATA lv_pq_name      TYPE pq_qname.
DATA lv_idx          TYPE sytabix.
*DATA ir_or_item                 TYPE REF TO apoc_s_or_item.
FIELD-SYMBOLS <ls_msg> TYPE cl_somu_form_services=>ty_gs_message.
TYPES:
  BEGIN OF ty_gs_fdp_key,
    name  TYPE string,
    value TYPE string,
  END OF ty_gs_fdp_key .
TYPES:
  ty_gt_fdp_key TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_gs_fdp_key WITH EMPTY KEY .
DATA: ls_master_keys    TYPE ty_gt_fdp_key, wa_ls_master_keys TYPE ty_gs_fdp_key.
DATA: ls_appl_keys    TYPE ty_gt_fdp_key, wa_ls_appl_keys TYPE ty_gs_fdp_key.
DATA _fcode        TYPE t185f-fcode.
DATA(lo_fact_int)  = cl_apoc_or_h_factory_internal=>get_helper_factory( ).
DATA(lo_form_util) = lo_fact_int->get_forms_utility( ).
DATA(lo_or_h_util) = lo_fact_int->get_apoc_or_h_utility_instance( ).
DATA(lo_chk_proxy) = lo_fact_int->get_or_check_proxy( cl_apoc_or_check_proxy=>gc_channel_check_item_minimum ).
DATA(lo_log)       = lo_fact_int->get_apoc_logger( iv_name = if_apoc_logger=>gc_apoc_main_logger_name ).
DATA(lo_log_audit) = lo_fact_int->get_apoc_logger( iv_name = 'AUDITOR_TRACE' ).
DATA(lo_auth_srv)  = lo_fact_int->get_authorization_service( ).
DATA(lo_ch_srv)    = lo_fact_int->get_or_item_channel_serv( ).
DATA(lo_at_srv)    = lo_fact_int->get_apoc_attachment_service( ).
DATA(lo_doc_fact)  = lo_fact_int->get_document_factory( ).
DATA(lo_cfg)       = cl_apoc_services_factory=>get_config_instance( ).
DATA eo_message  TYPE REF TO /bobf/if_frw_message.
eo_message         = lo_fact_int->get_bobf_message( ).

DATA: go_pdf_html_control TYPE REF TO cl_gui_html_viewer,
      go_html_container   TYPE REF TO cl_gui_custom_container.
PARAMETERS po_nr TYPE ebeln.
PARAMETERS pdf_form TYPE  fpwbformname DEFAULT 'MM_PUR_PURCHASE_ORDER'.
PARAMETERS pdf_mfor TYPE apoc_form_master_template_id DEFAULT 'APOC_DEMO_FORM_MASTER_CA'.

wa_ls_master_keys-name = 'PrintFormDerivationRule'.
wa_ls_master_keys-value = 'DCP_PURCHASE_ORDER_01'.
APPEND wa_ls_master_keys TO ls_master_keys.
wa_ls_master_keys-name = 'WatermarkText'.
wa_ls_master_keys-value = 'Test Copy'.
APPEND wa_ls_master_keys TO ls_master_keys.
wa_ls_master_keys-name = 'LocaleCountry'.
wa_ls_master_keys-value = 'CA'.
APPEND wa_ls_master_keys TO ls_master_keys.
wa_ls_master_keys-name = 'LocaleLanguage'.
wa_ls_master_keys-value = 'E'.
APPEND wa_ls_master_keys TO ls_master_keys.
wa_ls_master_keys-name = 'OutputControlApplicationObjectType'.
wa_ls_master_keys-value = 'PURCHASE_ORDER'.
APPEND wa_ls_master_keys TO ls_master_keys.
wa_ls_master_keys-name = 'OutputControlApplicationObject'.
*wa_ls_master_keys-value = '4500000369'.
wa_ls_master_keys-value = po_nr.
APPEND wa_ls_master_keys TO ls_master_keys.
wa_ls_master_keys-name = 'OutputRequestItem'.
*wa_ls_master_keys-value = '000002'.
APPEND wa_ls_master_keys TO ls_master_keys.
wa_ls_master_keys-name = 'OutputDocumentType'.
wa_ls_master_keys-value = 'PURCHASE_ORDER'.
APPEND wa_ls_master_keys TO ls_master_keys.
wa_ls_master_keys-name = 'Recipient'.
wa_ls_master_keys-value = ''.
APPEND wa_ls_master_keys TO ls_master_keys.
wa_ls_master_keys-name = 'RecipientRole'.
wa_ls_master_keys-value = ''.
APPEND wa_ls_master_keys TO ls_master_keys.
wa_ls_master_keys-name = 'SenderCountry'.
wa_ls_master_keys-value = 'CA'.
APPEND wa_ls_master_keys TO ls_master_keys.

wa_ls_appl_keys-name = 'PurchaseOrder'.
*wa_ls_appl_keys-value = '4500000369'.
wa_ls_appl_keys-value =  po_nr.
APPEND wa_ls_appl_keys TO ls_appl_keys.
wa_ls_appl_keys-name = 'SenderCountry'.
wa_ls_appl_keys-value = 'CA'.
APPEND wa_ls_appl_keys TO ls_appl_keys.
wa_ls_appl_keys-name = 'PurchaseOrderChangeFlag'.
wa_ls_appl_keys-value = ''.
APPEND wa_ls_appl_keys TO ls_appl_keys.
wa_ls_appl_keys-name = 'ReceiverPartnerNumber'.
wa_ls_appl_keys-value = ''.
APPEND wa_ls_appl_keys TO ls_appl_keys.
wa_ls_appl_keys-name = 'Language'.
wa_ls_appl_keys-value = 'E'.
APPEND wa_ls_appl_keys TO ls_appl_keys.


DATA render_options TYPE cl_somu_form_services=>ty_gs_render_options.
render_options-create_pdf = 'X'.
" Render Document
TRY.
    lo_hlp = cl_apoc_or_h_factory_internal=>get_helper_factory( ).
    " Get form provide services
    lo_fp_srv = lo_hlp->get_somu_form_services( ).

    lo_fp_srv->get_document(
      EXPORTING
*        iv_master_form_name     = 'ZZ1_DCP_MM_PUR_ORDER_MASTER'
        iv_master_form_name            = pdf_mfor
*        iv_form_name            = 'ZZ1_DCP_MM_PUR_PURCHASE_ORDER'
        iv_form_name            = pdf_form
        it_key                  = ls_appl_keys
*        it_filter_select_option = ls_appl-filter_options
        it_master_key           = ls_master_keys
        iv_form_language        = 'E'
        iv_form_country         = 'CA'
        is_render_options       = render_options
        iv_print_queue_name     = lv_pq_name
        iv_xml                  = lv_xml_in
        iv_disable_cache        = 'X'
      IMPORTING
        ev_content              = ls_merge_doc-document_data
        ev_pages                = ls_merge_doc-pages_count
        ev_stop_processing      = lv_stop
        et_message              = lt_msg
        ev_xml                  = lv_xml_out
        ev_pdl_type             = lv_pdl_type ).
    INSERT LINES OF lt_msg INTO TABLE lt_msg_all.

    IF ls_merge_doc-document_data IS NOT INITIAL.
      CALL SCREEN 0300 STARTING AT 1 1.
    ENDIF.

ENDTRY.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Module PBO_SET_STATUS_0300 OUTPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
MODULE pbo_set_status_0300 OUTPUT.
* SET PF-STATUS 'xxxxxxxx'.
* SET TITLEBAR 'xxx'.
  SET PF-STATUS 'STATUS_0300' EXCLUDING 'DOWN'.
ENDMODULE.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Module PBO_HTML_CONTROL OUTPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
MODULE pbo_html_control OUTPUT.
* SET PF-STATUS 'xxxxxxxx'.
* SET TITLEBAR 'xxx'.
  PERFORM html_control_pbo.
ENDMODULE.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form html_control_pbo
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& -->  p1        text
*& <--  p2        text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM html_control_pbo .
* Container
  IF go_html_container IS INITIAL.
    CREATE OBJECT go_html_container
      EXPORTING
        container_name = 'HTML'
      EXCEPTIONS
        OTHERS         = 1.
    IF sy-subrc IS NOT INITIAL.
      " MESSAGE 'CONTROL ERROR' TYPE 'E'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

* HTML control
  IF go_pdf_html_control IS INITIAL.
    CREATE OBJECT go_pdf_html_control
      EXPORTING
        parent = go_html_container
      EXCEPTIONS
        OTHERS = 1.
    IF sy-subrc IS NOT INITIAL.
      " MESSAGE 'CONTROL ERROR' TYPE 'E'.
    ENDIF.

*    IF gs_or_item-print_queue_name NE if_apoc_or_c=>gc_download_form_data_xml.
    PERFORM html_control_display_pdf.
*    ELSE.
*      PERFORM html_control_display_xml.
*    ENDIF.

  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form html_control_display_pdf
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& -->  p1        text
*& <--  p2        text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM html_control_display_pdf .
  DATA: lv_url      TYPE char80,
        lv_pdf_data TYPE tsfixml,
        lv_pdf_size TYPE i.

* Convert output data to binary format
  CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'
    EXPORTING
      buffer     = ls_merge_doc-document_data
    TABLES
      binary_tab = lv_pdf_data.
  lv_pdf_size = xstrlen( ls_merge_doc-document_data ).

* Load output data
  CALL METHOD go_pdf_html_control->load_data
    EXPORTING
      url          = 'preview.pdf'  "#EC NOTEXT
      size         = lv_pdf_size
      type         = 'application'  "#EC NOTEXT
      subtype      = 'pdf'
    IMPORTING
      assigned_url = lv_url
    CHANGING
      data_table   = lv_pdf_data
    EXCEPTIONS
      OTHERS       = 1.
  IF sy-subrc IS NOT INITIAL.
    MESSAGE ID     'APOC_OR_UI_MESSAGES'
            TYPE   'E'
            NUMBER '052'.
  ENDIF.

* Show output data
  CALL METHOD go_pdf_html_control->show_data
    EXPORTING
      url    = lv_url
    EXCEPTIONS
      OTHERS = 1.
  IF sy-subrc IS NOT INITIAL.
    MESSAGE ID     'APOC_OR_UI_MESSAGES'
            TYPE   'E'
            NUMBER '053'.
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Module  PAI_HTML_CONTROL  INPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
MODULE pai_html_control INPUT.
  PERFORM html_control_pai.
ENDMODULE.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form html_control_pai
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& -->  p1        text
*& <--  p2        text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM html_control_pai .
  CASE _fcode.
    WHEN 'GOON' OR 'CANC'.
      IF NOT go_pdf_html_control IS INITIAL.
        go_pdf_html_control->free( ).
        FREE go_pdf_html_control.
      ENDIF.
      IF NOT go_html_container IS INITIAL.
        go_html_container->free( ).
        FREE go_html_container.
      ENDIF.
      LEAVE TO SCREEN 0.
    WHEN 'DOWN'.
*      PERFORM download_xml.
    WHEN OTHERS.
      cl_gui_cfw=>dispatch( ).
  ENDCASE.

  CLEAR _fcode.
ENDFORM.

entry.title: Displaying Completed LMS Items in Employee File
entry.content:
Hi All,

How can we display completed LMS items (Learning History courses) in the Employee File?

I have reviewed the Configure People Profile settings but couldn’t find any relevant LMS blocks.

Best regards,
Omar

entry.title: HANA ODBC Test Connection Error
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm currently trying to establish an ODBC connection to a SAP HANA Cloud database, I need this to connect the data to a Microsoft PowerBI report. However, no matter what I try, the following message appears everytime I try to test the connection:

"Communication Link Failure:-10709 connection failed (RTE:[89006] system call 'connect' failed, rc=10061, No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it)."

Please consider the following:

  • My computer can successfully ping the SAP Hana server I'm trying to connect to, via cmd.
  • The credentials needed to test the ODBC connection are disregarded, it makes no difference if I use the correct credentials or purposefully wrong ones, the connection will show the same error.

Any help or advice is greatly appreciated.

Thanks,

Daniel.

entry.title: Library Browse improvement: Can we customize the order in which trainings are displayed the library?
entry.content:
Users are not able to find trainings they are looking for in LMS. The sort by option in the Library does not let us sort the order in which trainings tagged with certain categories show up. We have ongoing online webinars every month that we launch in LMS but users are not able to see them. Older webinars from many years ago show up on the top. This applies for trainings. We need to be able to order these library searches based on when they were launched so it is chronological. In addition we should be able to set other ways we want to order the library searches. 

Enhancement ticket: Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence

Can you please expedite this ticket or let us know if there is a way to use the Sort by option in the library to display search results chronologically or in any order we see fit for us?

entry.title: UX, AI, SAP S/4HANA transformation sessions at SAP Sapphire and ASUG Annual Conference 2025
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire and the ASUG Annual Conference are a great way to see the art of the possible, get inspired, and take away some concrete next steps on how to move your organization forward. Innovations in AI are making complex ERP systems easier to use and increasing the urgency for moving to the cloud, where most of these AI advances are available. In this post, I’ll list some of the sessions I’m most excited about, grouped by AI, SAP Fiori UX, moving to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and personal development, including organizational change management. As always, you can find the full list of sessions in the SAP Sapphire & ASUG Annual Conference Orlando session catalog.

 

AI Sessions

AI plays an increasing role in simplifying both user experience (UX) and application development. Embedded AI in SAP Fiori elements applications helps people find information more easily and share it with their colleagues. SAP’s pro-code development tools use AI to generate apps from a document or image and allow you update your app using natural language. These sessions provide an overview of how to start generating value from AI.

ASUG104 | Harnessing SAP's AI Innovations: Joule, Generative AI, and Business AI
Explore SAP's AI advancements, including Joule, the generative AI copilot embedded across SAP solutions. Discover practical use cases to establish a strong data foundation for AI deployment. Learn responsible AI governance and compliance strategies to ensure ethical, secure, and effective integration in your organization.
(Half-day ASUG pre-conference session. 1:00-5:00pm)

BAI1616 | AI in 2025: Thrive with SAP Business AI
Join us as we explore the evolution of AI and how SAP Business AI helps companies thrive in 2025. You can gain insights into AI advancements, better understand emerging trends, and learn how to engage with SAP to identify and implement custom AI solutions that drive success.
Bob Sakalas, Innovation Strategist, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 11:00 AM - 11:40 AM EDT

BAI1613 | Insights on cloud ERP and SAP Business AI from an SAP expert
Gain insight into SAP Business AI and its integration with our cloud ERP portfolio, including SAP S/4HANA. We explore current capabilities, upcoming features planned for 2025, and future innovations, while showing you how to adopt these capabilities and actively participate in shaping the future of enterprise technology.
Suresh Ramanathan, VP, Executive Advisor, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 1:00 PM - 1:40 PM EDT

BTP2574 | Create AI agents with Project Agent Builder and AI agent runtime on SAP BTP
With Project Agent Builder on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), you can simplify AI agent creation, streamline integration, and maintain compliance and security. The agents support complex tasks with multistep planning, reasoning, and explainability. Explore real-world use cases to see how agents enhance decision-making and efficiency.
Benjamin Stoekhert, Head of BTP AI Engineering, SAP
Jochen Schneider, Head of SAP BTP AI, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 3:00 PM - 3:40 PM EDT

ERP2185 | Boost your productivity with AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private leverages generative AI to increase end-user productivity and drive business process efficiency while helping ensure adherence to enterprise integrity and guardrails. Get a preview of how the processes are augmented by the use of agentic AI frameworks in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

ERP2190 | SAP Business AI and Joule in action with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
Take a look at task automation and conversational interactions across various lines of business. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition uses AI agents in our embedded Joule technology to increase people's productivity, automate business processes, and improve decision-making.

 

User experience or SAP Fiori sessions

SAP Fiori makes users and developers more productive. Below are several sessions where customers share their experience with SAP Fiori and the business benefits they have generated with a more intuitive UX. We also have content on the design system, so you can build your own apps that look and behave like the ones that SAP ships.

 

ERP1612 | Enhancing user experience with SAP Fiori and AI
Using AI in SAP Fiori apps can enhance productivity and collaboration. Learn best practices for SAPUI5, SAP Fiori elements, and tools to build enterprise-grade apps efficiently. Discover practical tips to deploy apps quickly, leveraging AI, and bring your questions for a deep dive into innovative solutions.
Peter Spielvogel, SAP Cloud ERP UX Foundation, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 11:00 AM - 11:40 AM EDT

ERP2100 | Naturipe's journey: SAP GUI to SAP Fiori with RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Naturipe Farms LLC upgraded to SAP S/4HANA in 2018, replacing SAP GUI with SAP Fiori launchpad. This became the central hub for functionality as more apps moved to the SAP Fiori UX and SAPUI5. Learn how transitioning from ABAP to CDS views, managed database procedures, and the ABAP RESTful application programming model streamlined data access.
Tuesday, May 20. 2:00 PM - 2:40 PM EDT

HCM1611 | SAP Fiori to SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone: Duval Schools overhauls the UX
Learn how Duval County Public Schools is enhancing its digital transformation by implementing the SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone solution for a smooth employee UX. Building on the current SAP Fiori launchpad, SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone provides tools for daily tasks, such as updating personal information, viewing payroll, and requesting PTO.
Tuesday, May 20. 3:00 PM - 3:40 PM EDT

ERP1610 | VistaPrint's journey using SAP S/4HANA and SAP Fiori to innovate
E-commerce company VistaPrint is leveraging SAP S/4HANA to drive business transformation, maintain system cleanliness, and unlock innovation. Discover strategies for staying current with SAP S/4HANA innovations and scaling SAP Fiori apps to enhance user experiences, optimize operations, and deliver high business value across the enterprise.
Tuesday, May 20. 4:00 PM - 4:40 PM EDT

FIM2604 | Transforming the SAP S/4HANA software user experience with SAP Fiori apps
Utility company Nebraska Public Power District (NPPD) sought to maximize SAP S/4HANA software but faced adoption challenges. Using streamlined SAP Fiori apps and a human-centered approach of design thinking, agile delivery, and change management, it achieved faster troubleshooting and greater productivity, and set the stage for enterprise-wide innovation.
Wednesday, May 21. 11:30 AM - 12:10 PM EDT

PUX2821 | SAP's user-experience strategy to achieve better outcomes
Understand how SAP is leading the way to help business users get work done easily and with better outcomes. Our strategy provides a consistent and integrated suite-first experience, infuses AI nearly everywhere, makes it easier for our customers and partners to design and build great UX, and leverages our user research framework to meet your needs.
Arin Bhowmick, Chief Design Officer, SAP
Emil Voutta, Head of SAP Mobile Design System, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 3:30 PM - 3:50 PM EDT

PUX2825 | Suite-first experience: Seamless, consistent, and integrated
A suite-first approach elevates product and user experience by delivering consistency, integration, and efficiency across SAP solutions. Learn how the SAP Design System and central services create a seamless experience for web and mobile use cases, enhancing usability while helping ensure a cohesive and scalable product ecosystem for your business.
Arin Bhowmick, Chief Design Officer, SAP
Florian Heretsch, SVP, Head of Cross Engineering & Experience, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 1:00 PM - 1:20 PM EDT

PUX2826 | User experience innovations in our products: For today and tomorrow
Have a look at the latest UX innovations across our suite of applications, along with cool innovations coming soon. Examine SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA, as well as cloud products such as SAP SuccessFactors and SAP Ariba solutions. Also, check out selected highlights from UI technologies.
Tom Reiss, VP, SAP Fiori Product Management, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 4:30 PM - 4:50 PM EDT

PUX2827 | Creating a great UX for your own applications with SAP Design System
More easily create applications that look like those from SAP by using a powerful set of reusable components, patterns, and floor plans with the SAP Design System (SAP Fiori UX for web and for mobile), along with design guidelines on how to use them. Building applications is made easier through our AI-powered UI technologies for web and mobile apps.
Sophia Levens, Head of SAP Design System, SAP
Emil Voutta, Head of SAP Mobile Design System, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 1:30 PM - 1:50 PM EDT

 

Moving to SAP S/4HANA

Since we are delivering most of our AI innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, we want to make it as easy as possible for you to get there. For new customers, it’s easy; use SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for a ready-to-run cloud ERP system. For existing customers, there are different ways to upgrade, most of which involve moving to a clean core and using SAP Business Technology Platform. These sessions will help you plan and execute your move.

ASUG102 | Planning Your SAP S/4HANA Journey: From Cloud Choices To Change Management
Navigate SAP S/4HANA transformations in this full-day seminar. In our morning session, hear from SAP and customers on approaches, methodologies, and success stories. Then, choose your path to dive deeper: RISE and GROW with SAP applications or change management practices and tools to make a successful digital transformation.
Monday, May 19. 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM EDT

ERP2384 | Paths from SAP ECC to RISE with SAP: brownfield, greenfield, or bluefield
Customers looking to move to cloud ERP need the right path for their organization. What are the pitfalls? What are the advantages? Hear SAP experts, along with a panel of customers, conduct a Q&A on key considerations. Learn how your choice impacts your cloud ERP journey, cloud mindset, future adoption, and total cost of ownership.
Joseph Jones, Solution Advisor, S/4HANA Center of Excellence, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 3:00 PM - 3:40 PM EDT

ERP1672 | Mastering clean core for success with SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Customer panelists share their awareness, understanding, and adoption of SAP’s clean core approach. Explore how it helps address technical debt and what organizations need to support this change. Learn about SAP’s clean core recommendations for migration to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and join an open discussion to share insights.
Peter Keller, Chief Sovereign Cloud Architect, SAP
Wednesday, May 21. 11:30 AM - 12:10 PM EDT

SER2113 | Unlock the business value of clean core during your RISE with SAP journey
Join SAP experts and pioneering reference customers for a deeper dive on the clean core approach. You can learn more about the business value framework involved in a clean core strategy. Discover the tools that are available for your organization and how to best leverage them during your business transformation.

BTP2074 | Maximize your move to SAP cloud ERP and SAP Business Suite with SAP BTP
Accelerate your migration to SAP cloud ERP solutions and SAP Business Suite with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). With tools to migrate data and custom reports, and integration to connect applications, SAP BTP offers generative AI development to keep a clean ERP core, and a foundation to build, deploy, and manage AI-powered solutions.
Terry Penner, Product Expert, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 3:00 PM - 3:20 PM EDT

ERP1667 | RISE with SAP in action: Stories of transformation, benefits, and lessons
Hear how diverse organizations use the RISE with SAP solution to drive digital transformation, modernize cloud architecture, and unlock value. Panelists share candid lessons learned, benefits achieved, and challenges faced. Whether you’re considering or optimizing RISE with SAP, gain insights and actionable takeaways to maximize your deployment.
Kristen Scheffler, VP Customer Engagement North America, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 11:00 AM - 11:40 AM EDT

 

Personal development and organizational change management

With the pace of technological change moving so fast, it’s critical to stay informed about new developments and how to apply them. And to help your colleagues adopt new innovations to transform your business. These sessions will equip you with the knowledge and tools you need to be a change agent in your organization.

DIS1669 | Future-proof your career: Embrace lifelong learning at SAP
In today’s digital landscape, SAP professionals must stay ahead by upgrading their skills. Explore the importance of lifelong learning to remain relevant in the SAP ecosystem. You can discover strategies to adapt to rapid tech changes, leverage learning resources from SAP, and foster a culture of continuous education for long-term success.
Kristen Scheffler, VP Customer Engagement North America, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 11:00 AM - 11:40 AM EDT

TRE1605 | Why business transformation is a struggle—and how to fix it
Business transformation is rarely smooth. In fact, a recent SAP and Oxford Economics study found that only half of transformation initiatives succeed. What separates success from failure? Explore real-world data and hear how emerging technologies like AI can help you set goals, gain management buy-in, and identify risks before they derail progress.
Myrna Acosta, Chief Business Officer, SAP Business Transformation Management, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 2:00 PM - 2:20 PM EDT

BTM1666 | Mastering the art of organizational change management
In SAP software implementations, successful change management is like baking the perfect pie: every ingredient matters. Learn about the essential steps for a smooth transition, from preparing your team to embracing new processes. Hear how to blend communication, training, and leadership into a recipe for lasting implementation success.
Kristen Scheffler, VP Customer Engagement North America, SAP
Wednesday, May 21. 11:30 AM - 12:10 PM EDT

SER2114 | From “A” to “Impact”: Measuring business value with AI
Explore how we use data-driven insights and process mining to create a measurable business impact with our AI scenarios. You can find out how we create a framework to measure and validate value generation and gain actionable insights so that you can improve your business outcomes in an agentic AI world.

I’m excited to have conversations with customers and partners about these topics, both as a representative of SAP Cloud ERP UX Engineering and the ASUG point of contact for user experience and public cloud. Please reach out to me to schedule a time to meet or you can arrange this through your account executive.

Hope to see you there.

entry.title: Re: Try use JavaScript scripting instead of Groovy scripting in your CPI project
entry.content:
Hey Lionel, and thanks for the post!

Regarding the "what is the difference between these possibile version no": back in the old days browsers could have their own versions of JS implemented in their engines (some might even remember rather "special" flavour of JS in MSIE) and so Mozilla Firefox had its own stuff as well.

For example, here at the very bottom we still can find release notes for versions 1.5, 2 and 3 relating to certain JS versions of their JS engine (most likely SpiderMonkey).

So, if I am not mistaken, those versions  100, ..., 170, 180 for rhino would correspond to Mozilla's own JS implementations (which could be aligned to spidermonkey).

This page (rather obsolete) for example mentions 1.5 and it features for rhino, while this one (from wayback machine) - 1.7 (which was the version mentioned in Firefox 2 release).

And in general features seem to match.

 

What makes it important?

Well, even though the engine version 1.7R4 is rather old indeed, looks like some features of JS that were introduced in ECMA only much later were already there ("Firefox 2 supports JavaScript 1.7, which includes new features including let, destructuring assignment, generators and iterators, and array comprehensions." - how cool is that?!)

This is why, surprisingly, you can find those features "marked green" in the comparison table for rhino here.

Another important thing to mention is it seems that version 160 introduced lots of stuff we know and love plus https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ECMAScript_for_XML

Also there is some stackoverflow answer basically summarizing the same stuff I just wrote.

 

In short, imo 180 seems to be modern enough especially for our purposes (just don't expect npm packages, fetch api and promises/async-await api here)

Hope this helps someone 😉

P.S. Looks like reduce was only introduced in 1.8 though, which I find rather funny )

entry.title: Steps to configure Bitcoin as the functional currency in SAP
entry.content:
A legal entity that prepares its financial statements in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) must adhere to IAS 21 IAS 21 — The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates and IAS 29 IAS 29 — Financial Reporting in Hyperinflationary Economies. These standards mandate the use of a functional and/or reporting currency for financial statements. In SAP, ISO - ISO 4217 — Currency codes are available by default and can be utilized as functional and/or reporting currencies. But cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin are not listed yet!

The following configuration allows Bitcoin to be used as a currency in SAP:

  1. Transaction OY03 - Configure BTC as a currency.
  2. Settings for translating from local currency (EUR) to BTC.
    1. Transaction OBBS - Configure translation ratios.
    2. Transaction OB08 - Maintain exchange rate
  3. Company Code settings
    1. Transaction OX15 - Define internal trading partner and assign BTC as the currency.
    2. Transaction FINSC_LEDGER

Currency Conversion Settings for Company Codes

Company Code Settings for the Ledger 0L

Bitcoin (BTC) is now configured in Accounting and can be used as both a functional and reporting currency. To report balance sheet accounts, vendor accounts, and customer accounts in BTC, the currency type representing BTC must be assigned to the appropriate valuation area using Transaction S_AL0_19000080.

To report fixed assets in BTC, the currency type representing BTC values must be assigned to the depreciation area using Transaction OAYH.

DM&LT offers a currency conversion service to convert systems to new currency target setup. See blog  Data Management and Landscape Transformations - Cu... - SAP Community and Data Unification & Harmonization

With these basic configuration, an entity can report its financial statements in Bitcoin (BTC). However, fully integrating Bitcoin into SAP requires process redesign and enhancements. Consider the following areas:

Purchase-to-Pay

  • Payments to external vendors and affiliated companies in Bitcoin

Hire-to-Retire

  • Employees can be compensated in Bitcoin.
  • Taxes can be paid in Bitcoin.

Order-to-Cash

  • Retailers can accept Bitcoin. How to receive and invoice Bitcoin in SAP 
  • Billing customers in Bitcoin.
  • Clear and settle of open customer items which are paid in Bitcoin.

Record-to-Report
With Bitcoin as an asset in the financial statement, auditors and investors can easily validate Bitcoin balances (UTXO) via their own Bitcoin node or through third-party APIs, such as the mempool.space API. 

To prove ownership of a Bitcoin address, the directors of an legal entity should be able to sign a message. Here are the steps:

  1. Generate a Message: Create a unique message, such as "I own this address [timestamp]".
  2. Sign the Message: Use the private key associated with the Bitcoin address to sign the message. This can be done using wallets like Bitcoin Core, Electrum, or hardware wallets like Ledger and Trezor.
  3. Verify the Signature: Provide the signed message, the signature, and the Bitcoin address to the interested party. They can use the public key to verify the signature and confirm ownership without exposing your private key.

SAP Landscape Transformation SAP Business Data Cloud

Example code for checking a Bitcoin wallet balance via API access:

DATA: lv_url TYPE string,
      lv_address TYPE string VALUE '1A1zP1eP5QGefi2DMPTfTL5SLmv7DivfNa',
      lv_response TYPE string,
      lo_http_client TYPE REF TO if_http_client,
      lo_rest_client TYPE REF TO cl_rest_http_client,
      lt_utxos TYPE TABLE OF string,
      lv_utxo TYPE string.

" Define the API endpoint
lv_url = |https://mempool.space/api/address/{ lv_address }/utxo|.

" Create HTTP client instance
CALL METHOD cl_http_client=>create_by_url
  EXPORTING
    url                = lv_url
  IMPORTING
    client             = lo_http_client
  EXCEPTIONS
    argument_not_found = 1
    plugin_not_active  = 2
    internal_error     = 3
    OTHERS             = 4.

IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  WRITE: / 'Error creating HTTP client'.
  RETURN.
ENDIF.

" Send the request
CALL METHOD lo_http_client->send
  EXCEPTIONS
    http_communication_failure = 1
    http_invalid_state         = 2
    http_processing_failed     = 3
    OTHERS                     = 4.

IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  WRITE: / 'Error sending HTTP request'.
  RETURN.
ENDIF.

" Receive the response
CALL METHOD lo_http_client->receive
  IMPORTING
    data = lv_response
  EXCEPTIONS
    http_communication_failure = 1
    http_invalid_state         = 2
    http_processing_failed     = 3
    OTHERS                     = 4.

IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  WRITE: / 'Error receiving HTTP response'.
  RETURN.
ENDIF.

" Parse the JSON response
CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_JSON_TO_TABLE'
  EXPORTING
    json = lv_response
  TABLES
    json_table = lt_utxos.

" Output the UTXO details
LOOP AT lt_utxos INTO lv_utxo.
  WRITE: / lv_utxo.
ENDLOOP.

" Free the HTTP client
CALL METHOD lo_http_client->close.

entry.title: Re: Assignment profile LMS - prevent a training already l...
entry.content:
Hi @ChloeCoudray 

Currently, assignment profiles don't have a rule to consider the users' assigned items.

There have been some enhancements requests on it, and our customers are welcomed to vote for them and/or create additional requests:

  • https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/286766/?section=sectionDetails 

For more details on enhancement requests, please check the KBA 2090228 - How to submit enhancement ideas for SAP SuccessFactors Products.

However, as mentioned by @SF-Dan, the users will see only one instance of the item on their Learning Plan, even if it has been assigned more than once. This means the user only needs to complete the item once, and all assignments will be updated with the completion. More details at https://me.sap.com/notes/2603530

entry.title: Production order Co08 giving blank commits in Status
entry.content:
Hi all,

So in this requirement we need to update the sales order "Obj Status" with respect to Status of Plan Order (CO08 & CO02). To do this we are using BADI WORKORDER_UPDATE~ATSAVE. In the system the object status is maintained via a custom profile with is stored in JEST table. For that object all the active status are blank and inactive are X. 

 

I've written a code to do the modify, earlier using I_OBJECT_CHANGE, now Modify statment. After save i opened the update debugging I realised the commit happens after transaction is closed. But what it is doing is it is adding an extra empty line and since it's empty the inactive indicatior is false so it comes as active in system and no radio button is checked in sales order screen where this object status is displayed.

entry.title: Catalogue activation pre requisits and ANID
entry.content:
Hi all, 

Can some please share some knowledge regarding the pre requisits configuration to set on buyer´s side to activate supplier´s catalogue (external static and punch out) in Buyer Guided Buying?

entry.title: Introduction to Data Management with SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION:

This Academic Lesson exposes students to SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Datasphere, two tools to facilitate governed management, storage and sharing of an organization’s data from various sources. In the first Activity of the Academic Lesson, students will learn about the benefits of using SAP Business Data Cloud to connect to and prepare data for analysis. Through SAP Business Data Cloud, businesses have access to ready-to-use Insight Apps or may use machine learning or other mechanisms to analyze available business data. In the second Activity, students will learn more about how data from multiple sources may be stored and shared using SAP Datasphere, SAP’s public-cloud-based business data fabric.  SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Datasphere work together to provide businesses with reliable, user-friendly data analysis without the tedium of manual extraction and transformation. 

SUGGESTED AREAS OF STUDY:

Computer science, computer engineering, data analytics, data engineering, information systems, data managementAppropriate for students at undergraduate and graduate level university.  

DOWNLOAD:

https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/SZJS6A7?rc=10&doi=SAP1196258

entry.title: Re: Additional field on transportation unit (SAP S/4HANA)

entry.title: Designing Stories with SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION:

This Learning Journey Academic Lesson introduces students to the analytics required for decision making. Although the example in the lesson is one of a business operation, the same processes and theory may be used in just about any area of interest requiring data analysis. “Business” in the context of this document may be construed as the operations of the organization whether it be selling products or formulating chemical compounds or something else. The first activity of the lesson is an overview of the capabilities of SAP Analytics Cloud and the second activity dives deeply into creation of data “Stories”.

SUGGESTED AREAS OF STUDY:

Business (all majors), Information Systems, Social Sciences, Natural Sciences, Engineering, Medicine, Journalism, and other areas of study requiring analysis and visualization of data. Appropriate for students at undergraduate and graduate level university, community college, trade school and upper-level secondary school.  

DOWNLOAD: 

https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/keWdTC4?rc=10&doi=SAP1191525

The first activity of the lesson is an overview of the capabilities of SAP Analytics Cloud and the second activity dives deeply into the creation and population of models used in data analysis.

entry.title: Low-code/No-code Composition and Automation with SAP Build
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION:

This Academic Lesson explains how citizen developers can improve efficiency and streamline processes with low-code/no-code solutions, specifically SAP Build. Students are introduced to app building, process automation, and web development that provides an organization’s users with centralized access to productivity assets that make their work more efficient. The importance of governance “guard rails” for citizen developers is also introduced in this Academic Lesson.

Activity 1 of this Academic Lesson utilizes simulated activities in SAP Build to walk students through the steps of app building, process automation, and web development. The simulations prompt the students to make the appropriate entries into the system to complete various tasks in SAP Build. Activity 2 explains the steps for SAP Build Apps in more detail so that students can build their own app. At the end of this Academic Lesson, the students will access a live SAP Build practice system to develop a personal health app.    

SUGGESTED AREAS OF STUDY:

Any area of study that introduces programming and app development. Some examples are information systems, information technology, computer science, computer engineering, and business. Students in advanced courses in other areas such as public administration, health care, real estate, and education may also benefit from this lesson. Appropriate for students at undergraduate and graduate level university, community college, trade school, and upper-level secondary school.

DOWNLOAD:

https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/P5avkTV?rc=10&doi=SAP1191519

entry.title: Financial Accounting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION:

This Academic Lesson explains how accounting-related business transactions are processed, managed, and reported in an ERP system, specifically SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Students will experience management of an organization’s accounts payable, accounts receivable, and asset accounting functions and observe the full financial accounting cycle from data entry of transactions to close of the accounting period to form the financial statements. 

SUGGESTED AREAS OF STUDY:

Business (all majors), Information Systems, and other areas of study teaching basic business accounting or ERP systems knowledge. Appropriate for students at undergraduate and graduate level university, community college, trade school, and upper-level secondary school.

DOWNLOAD:

https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/zUK4wqR?rc=10&doi=SAP1191516

entry.title: Supply Chain Processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION:

The first part of this Academic Lesson introduces the concept of cloud computing. The second part of the Academic Lesson provides an overview of supply chain processes in a typical business. It focuses on inventory management, order processing, logistics material identification, and delivery management, but also identifies warehouse management and transportation management as part of the supply chain. Warehouse and transportation management are the topics of other Learning Journeys. This Learning Journey also introduces the concept of the organizational elements and enterprise structure required for an ERP system to process and report transactions according to business requirements.

SUGGESTED AREAS OF STUDY:

Business (all majors), Information Systems, and others that require an understanding of how to procure goods and services in an ERP system. Appropriate for students at undergraduate and graduate level university, community college, trade school and upper-level secondary school.  

DOWNLOAD:

https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/G6mpTwd?rc=10&doi=SAP1191511

entry.title: Human Capital Management Using SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION:

Human resource (HR) professionals are responsible for recruiting and staffing employees, employee compensation and benefits, employee relations, employee training and development, and compliance with local HR regulations. At the same time, they need to optimally manage human capital to help the organization meet its strategic goals. HR responsibilities can be complex and difficult to manage. SAP SuccessFactors assists HR professionals in all types of organizations to manage their duties and meet their goals.

This Academic Lesson introduces students to the technologies, specifically the SAP SuccessFactors capabilities, that enable employees and employers to process and track employment activities effectively. It will provide the students with a means of comparing the theory that they learn in class with the actual day-to-day processes that are part of human capital management and HR functions.

SUGGESTED AREAS OF STUDY:

Business - all majors and especially Management majors, Health Care Management, Public Administration, and other areas of study that teach employment and other human capital management topics. Appropriate for students at undergraduate and graduate level university, community college, and trade school.

DOWNLOAD:

https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/yBhtQ7h?rc=10&doi=SAP1191509

entry.title: Carbon Footprint Management in SAP Cloud for Sustainable Enterprises
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION:

This Academic Lesson explores the challenges of organizational climate action goals and regulatory requirements. It provides an introduction to the metrics of greenhouse gas emissions and carbon accounting. The Learning Journey describes the SAP solutions that assist companies in recording, monitoring, and reporting emissions and how those solutions provide the data and analysis tools to help companies meet emissions goals.

SUGGESTED AREAS OF STUDY:

Business – all areas, Information Systems, Agriculture, Manufacturing, Public Administration, Facilities Management, and other areas of study that include coverage of ESR sustainability topics. Appropriate for students at undergraduate and graduate level university, community college, and trade school.  

DOWNLOAD:

https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/TDmg7Zq?rc=10&doi=SAP1191505

entry.title: Re: How to attach the uploaded files of sap BPA to email
entry.content:
Hi @ayechan ,

If you are able to access the file in your process, then there are two ways, that I can think of to send the file through email:

1. Create an automation in the same project as your process workflow and call the automation as a notification step in between your process workflow. Within the automation, pass the dms file id and the dms destination. Using the dms destination and file id, create a copy of the file in the local system. Then use Outlook SDK to send the file to intended recipients.

2. You can create an automation in a different project, and then using API calls, access the specific instance of the workflow and extract DMS file id. Follow similar steps as mentioned in step 1.

Do let me know in case you wish for more information.

Best Regards,

Rohit

entry.title: The ABAP Detective Meets The Franklin Principle
entry.content:
Recently I conversed with a former colleague about travel plans, particularly how to get about an area with local maps and guides. They suggested to use a global corporation's mobile app, as it's "no extra charge" and ubiquitous. Yes, I countered, but I'd like to keep my privacy as much as possible, including switching off my mobile location data being fed into the global abyss.

Think about the scenario where the boss says "make sure none of our personal information is being saved in XYZ location data fields," and you realize those fitness, navigation, and foody apps have been leaving radioactive bread crumb trails of you and your team's historic plodding. Oops. Poor governance by nation or company XYZ has now introduced unacceptable risk levels. How to comply with the newly evolved privacy rules? 

Google maps, the easy not so private approach:

Route map from G

The Three Map Problem; similar to the Four Color Map problem

Way back in my school days, we learned of the traveling sales person route optimization, using simplex method linear programming [ https://www.britannica.com/topic/simplex-method ]. Find the shortest or fastest route to visit one or more locations.

Are we safe keeping location data in the cloud? In the story https://www.yahoo.com/news/dutch-parliament-calls-end-reliance-155717872.html, Bert Hubert, a Dutch technology expert who has advocated for reducing dependency on the U.S., said "But he said one important outcome would be forcing agencies to publicly report on risks related to their reliance on U.S. cloud firms."

As Benjamin Franklin said, "Those who would give up essential Liberty, to purchase a little temporary Safety, deserve neither Liberty nor Safety." In this case, we will keep the liberty of our privacy at the cost of building our own repository, or just not collecting data we don't want leaked.

At the time of the 2020 U.S. Census, I was recruited to be an enumerator, given an Apple phone, and sent out to collect personal data from citizens and residents. The tech behind the data collection was designed so that a field agent could record answers quickly on a mobile device, after which the content was inaccessible. With an always-on GPS, the management level could tell where the agent went, the agent could be directed from service point to service point, and the agent could not feasibly leak anything significant. 

Fast forward to a new challenge--keeping a personal map of available vehicle recharging points, avoiding over-sharing with others, and getting directions when needed. The vehicle has mapping software, along with a built-in GPS, and is sourced from the manufacturer, not Google or Apple or Microsoft. Having a mobile device with another GPS source gives a second choice for location routing; adding an open source app then gives 3 navigation choices. We'll ignore the added risk to compliance if another onboard device is added, say from an occupant's mobile, or, worse, an "air tag" secreted on the vehicle for third party tracking/sleuthing.

The Simplex project I once worked on was for fire station location; using a University to present community resource shutdown options let the government officials offload political decisions to external experts and duck responsibility. Unlike a sales rep missing a call, a too-late fire or emergency response has life and health risk levels.

Without revealing any private info, including current location, can an app with preloaded map data compare to
"live" services supplied by cloud computing? How much power is used "at hand" to complete complex routing
and visible/audible guidance ("speed bump ahead")? Crowd-sourced/open-sourced data compared to private caches and compared to "learning machines" (read street signs, speed limit, real time obstructions/road conditions / hazards?

After searching for available tools, I chose the OsmAnd app for verification of suitability for on-device navigation features. Crowd-sourcing the data and the algorithms was appealing. Free was a bonus (though as it turned out, the lower-tier no-cost option was restricted and unsuitable). Starting with field practice, I used known test paths first, adding points of interest incrementally. I could use a GIS app to create lists of favorite places, then load to the app as needed. For SAP ecosystem developers, the HANA database is supported by PostGIS (present on Windows QGIS but not all Linux or BSD packaging). Waypoint export was either a KML or a GPX file. Import to the navigation app was straightforward.

SAP HANA GIS data source

Map Test Drive (torture track)

I  tested the user interface, data quality, and suitability for general navigation in the map app. There were definitely oddities and idiosyncrasies, part of which could be explained by "it's different than the other apps" and part by quality control of nodes and routes. Of the 2 minor annoyances I found, both related to routing guidance. The first error was for a non-existent turn; the second I found was advice to "turn left" when the expected direction was "forward." Bonus features I found in OsmAnd were (surface water) stream direction and elevation profiles, and the default color scheme had excellent viewability.

Navigation panel with checkered flag. 

When I posted on Mastodon about a bad turn gripe, a mapping community member replied helpfully. Within a few minutes, they had tracked down the bogus interchange and submitted a fix to the OpenStreetMap project database. Yet to be determined: how long it takes for that update to be reflected on-device, as the app vendor has multiple tiers of service level. Could be a month before new maps show the correction. Is this adequate response time for general navigation? As usual, that depends on the requirement.

Illustration: Stream flow in 2 directions in a drainage ditch:

The second major glitch I found was incorrect guidance to "turn left" when the route should not have a turn. In order to best report the error to someone who could help fix it, I went back to advice I was given to filing bug report: create a reproducible test case with minimum details, nothing extraneous. Because I saw nothing obviously wrong myself, I varied the planned start/end points, getting the same error. Good progress. Then I repeated the trip, trying to gather in situ details to determine what input data caused the invalid calculation. I also searched for similar errors ("roundabout entry/exit mangled") online, to get a clearer sense of possible variances. My working assumption at this point is the highway has turn-only lanes approaching the error point, such that the guidance should more properly be "bear left" due to a right-turn-only lane. I'm close enough to file a bug report that can be acted on, or coerce someone to correct the data anomaly causing the glitch.

Turn suggestions, three little arrows, one bold.

Private Property Concerns

A related barrier to trusting crowd-source geo data is whether a planned route, or endpoint, is accessible. One of the chargers a search revealed turned out to be within a paid parking lot. Can't say for sure whether this is a contributor error, or data evolution ("it was reachable on the side of the road until someone put up a fence").

Related, navigation and destination data might be considered as "inclusive" or "exclusive" domains. You might need to know if your location is within certain boundaries, i.e. within 50 miles of a US national border, in or out of the immigration search zone. Or you might need to prove you *were not* there, within reasonable doubt.

  1. Keep data ~outside~ specific areas (quarantine zones)
  2. ~within~ specific areas (safe harbors / embassies)

Country or region specific rules require data be kept per above. Or, concerns about leaks to competitors, government TLA's, or bad actors. Or, just don't want to do business with Company or Country "XYZ", .e.g. boycott. Might need to follow embargo rules such as: don't do business with Cuba, Libya, North Korea, or wherever the rules of the day state.

Conclusions

Deploying an on-device, no-data-transfer mapping app is feasible, should one wish or be required to prevent privacy data leaks. Comparing huge tech company apps to much smaller open source based code and data shows a small loss of privacy freedom can be overcome by the larger feeling of safety in relative anonymity.

To do:

  1. Try navigating with cell data off (first results - OsmAnd worked great; Google maps still worked by complained about not being able to show current traffic conditions. Noted).
  2. Review data transferred (while using mobile data, see what leaks?)
  3. Measure power used (tricky, because the current scenario requires the mobile device to be plugged into a USB to allow Android Auto to work, so battery activity tracking is moot).

References

  • OSMand
  • QGis
  • OpenStreetMap
  • Striking a Balance: Navigating the Open-Source Tightrope in a World of Evolving Threats - Part 2
  •

entry.title: Re: Query to show "Production Order" and related "Issue f...
entry.content:
Hi @azacc ,

Try this query, might need to be adjusted to fit your requierment

SELECT DISTINCT T1."DocEntry", T0."DocNum", T0."DocDate", 
T0."CardName", T1."ItemCode", T1."Dscription",  T1."unitMsr", T6."ExpDate",  T5."BatchNum", 
T5."WhsCode", T4."BinCode",T4."SL1Code",
T3."Quantity",T9."InvntryUom"
FROM OWOR A
left join WOR1 B on A."DocEntry"=B."DocEntry"
left JOIN IGE1 T1 ON B."DocEntry" = T1."BaseEntry" and B."LineNum" = T1."BaseLine" and T1."BaseType"=202
left join OIGE T0 on T0."DocEntry"=T1."DocEntry"
LEFT JOIN OILM T2 ON T1."DocEntry" = T2."DocEntry" AND T1."ObjType" = T2."TransType" AND T1."ItemCode" = T2."ItemCode" AND T2."LocCode" = T1."WhsCode"
LEFT JOIN OBTL T3 ON T2."MessageID" = T3."MessageID"
LEFT JOIN OBIN T4 ON T3."BinAbs" = T4."AbsEntry"
LEFT JOIN IBT1 T5 ON T1."ObjType" = T5."BaseType" AND T1."ItemCode" = T5."ItemCode" AND T1."DocEntry" = T5."BaseEntry" AND T1."LineNum" = T5."BaseLinNum" AND T1."WhsCode" = T5."WhsCode"
LEFT JOIN OBTN T6 ON T6."DistNumber" = T5."BatchNum" 
left JOIN ITL1 T8 ON T6."SysNumber" = T8."SysNumber" AND T1."ItemCode" = T8."ItemCode" AND T3."SnBMDAbs" = T8."MdAbsEntry"

INNER JOIN OITM T9 ON T9."ItemCode" = T1."ItemCode"

Regards,

Paul

entry.title: ASEエラーログ情報をEWAで確認できるか
entry.content:
過去、DM1でprocedure cache 不足により 新規DAIプロセスが受け付けられず、 GUIで接続できない状態になりました。
その時、MMCのdisp+work のステータスが黄色になりなっており、インスタンス再起動しても 接続できなかったので、OS再起動を行いました。
--
server  Error: 701, Severity: 17, State: 10
server  There is not enough procedure cache to run this procedure, trigger, or SQL batch. Retry later, or ask your SA to reconfigure ASE with more procedure cache.
--

今後 procedure cache 不足が起こらないように、毎日 Tr-cd:DB02でASEエラーログ情報を確認していますが、他の確認方法がないか探しています。
毎週EWAを取得しているので、ASEエラーログ情報をEWAで確認できるかを知りたいです。


English:

In the past, DM1 could not accept new DAI processes due to insufficient procedure cache, and it became impossible to connect via the GUI.

At that time, the status of disp+work in MMC was yellow, and even restarting the instance did not allow connection, so I restarted the OS.

--
server  Error: 701, Severity: 17, State: 10
server  There is not enough procedure cache to run this procedure, trigger, or SQL batch. Retry later, or ask your SA to reconfigure ASE with more procedure cache.
--

To prevent a procedure cache shortage in the future, I check the ASE error log information on Tr-cd:DB02 every day, but I am looking for another way to check it.

I get EWA every week, so I would like to know if I can check the ASE error log information on EWA.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: ASEエラーログ情報をEWAで確認できるか
entry.content:
ASEエラーログでエラーが発生した場合、同じエラー情報をEWAで確認する事ができません。
EWAを通じて直接的にASEエラーログ情報を確認することは難しいかもしれませんが、
EWAレポートのデータベースに関する解析セクションと他のモニタリングツール（DB02、DBACOCKPITなど）の組み合わせにより、
データベースの健全性を維持するための包括的な監視体制を構築することができます。 


The above ASE error log cannot be viewed in EWA.

It may be difficult to check the ASE error log information directly through EWA,
but by combining the database analysis section of the EWA report with other monitoring tools (DB02, DBACOCKPIT, etc.),
you can build a comprehensive monitoring system to maintain the health of your database.
